original_txt:
Chicago health care workers cheer patients released after beating COVID19Advocate Aurora Health discharged its 1000th COVID19 patient this week a landmark that health care workers at the hospital system are celebrating by spotlighting the many individual victories behind that figure

At Advocate Illinois Masonic Medical Center in Lake View Carmen Benabe 86 was released to quarantine at home four days after being admitted to the emergency room with a COVID19 diagnosis according to a release from the hospital system Benabes daughter Dalia Colon said the family is thankful in a statement and that her mom is looking forward to enjoying homecooked Puerto Rican meals again soon

At Advocate Trinity Hospital on the South Side Paul Richards 69 a retired Chicago firefighter and a Vietnam veteran headed home amid a standing ovation passing handmade posters that read GOINGHOME after he spent two weeks on a ventilator for COVID19 symptoms

It was like being in Vietnam Richards said in a release from the hospital Everyone responded with no hesitation Everyone stepped up to do what they had to do and put their lives on the line to care for me

Dr Gary Stuck chief medical officer of Advocate Aurora Health said he was grateful for the service and sacrifice of doctors and nurses at the hospital system emphasized that theres more work to be done for medical staff and for Chicagoans According to the latest figures from the state Illinois has now seen 868 deaths and 23247 coronavirus cases

Related Live map of Illinois coronavirus cases

The public must continue to do their part by practicing social distancing and staying home as much as possible Stuck said in a release There will be more milestones to come both happy and tragic And our actions now will determine how much celebrating or grieving we do later Were all in this together
corr_txt:
Chicago health care workers observe patients discharged after COVID-19 recovery at Advocate Aurora Health, marking a significant milestone as the system released its thousandth patient of the pandemic. This achievement has prompted expressions of gratitude and recognition for the collective effort in overcoming this public health crisis within the hospital network's Lake View Carmen facility.

At Advocate Illinois Masonic Medical Center, Benabe family members expressed their happiness as Dalia Colon’s mother was released to quarantine at home after two weeks of treatment and ventilation for COVID-19 symptoms in a statement shared by the hospital system. The retired Chicago firefighter is expected to enjoy his homemade Puerto Rican meals again soon, amidst heartfelt thanks from family members who were present during her care.

Meanwhile at Advocate Trinity Hospital on the South Side, Paul Richards was released after spending two weeks in intensive treatment and ventilation for COVID-19 symptoms; his release prompted a standing ovation as he passed handmade posters that read "Going Home." Reflecting upon this experience at Advocate Aurora Health's Stuck Center, Richards expressed admiration for the unwavering response of everyone who stepped up to provide care during such challenging times.

Dr Gary Stuck emphasized in a release from Advocate Illinois Masonic Medical Center that while there is still work left to do both medically and socially—such as practicing social distancing, staying home whenever possible, continuing the fight against COVID-19 now will determine how much celebrating or grieving we'll have later. He emphasized our collective responsibility in this ongoing battle for public health within Chicago’s communities.
